{{Short description|2015 Czech historical reality show}}
{{italic title}}
{{use dmy dates |date=March 2022}}
'''''Holiday in the Protectorate''''' ({{langx|cs|Dovolená v protektorátu}}) was a 2015 historical [[reality TV show]] created by [[Czech Television]], the public television broadcaster in the [[Czech Republic]],<ref name="day">{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/news/worldnews/europe/czechrepublic/11613218/Czech-producers-criticised-over-reality-TV-show-depicting-family-living-under-Nazi-occupation.html|title=Czech producers criticised over reality TV show depicting family living under 'Nazi occupation'|last=Day|first=Matthew |date=18 May 2015|work=[[The Daily Telegraph]]|accessdate=22 April 2017}}</ref> which first aired on 23 May 2015. The title of the show refers to the [[Protectorate of Bohemia and Moravia]], the state established in the [[Czech lands]] following the [[Nazi occupation of Czechoslovakia]] in 1939, at the beginning of [[World War II]].<ref name="day"/> The plot of the show centered on a family placed in a cottage isolated in the middle of the forest under imaginary conditions of the restored protectorate. During their two-month stay in the cottage, the family had to face difficult conditions reminiscent of real wartime atmosphere.{{r|lyman}} They were threatened by attacks of [[Gestapo]] and are allowed only limited communication with outside world. Nazi soldiers and other characters in the show were played by hired actors.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/24/czech-reality-show-nazi-occupation-holiday-protectorate|title=Czech reality show recreates life under Nazi occupation |date=24 May 2015 |work=[[The Guardian]]|accessdate=26 May 2015}}</ref> The show was criticized for its alleged trivialization of the suffering real wartime survivors.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.timesofisrael.com/czech-reality-tv-show-relives-nazi-occupation/|title=Czech show relives Nazi occupation|last=Ghert-Zand|first=Renee |date=21 May 2015|work=[[Times of Israel]]|accessdate=26 May 2015}}</ref>

==Background==
The show was conceived by documentary maker Zora Cejnkova,<ref>{{Cite web | url=http://www.kmb-intl.com/Movies/Detail/id/33 | title=KMB International Films}}</ref> inspired by [[Channel 4]]'s ''[[The 1940s House]]'', [[Philip Zimbardo]]'s 1970s [[Stanford prison experiment]] and the [[BBC]]'s ''[[The Experiment]]''.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} Czech psychologist {{ill|Slavomil Hubalek|cs}} was consulted about the project, and he said that if they could find a family that was empathetic and communicated well with each other, the project could work if they were well isolated from the outside world. Cejnkova described the format as "Situation Drama" to distinguish it from [[reality television|reality shows]] such as ''[[Big Brother (franchise)|Big Brother]]''. The actors were required to improvise to keep the situations going, much in the way that Milgram used an actor in his 'torture' experiments in 1963.<ref name="ObedStudy">{{cite journal | last = Milgram | first = Stanley | year = 1963 | title = Behavioral Study of Obedience | journal = Journal of Abnormal and Social Psychology | volume = 67 | issue = 4 | pages = 371–8 | pmid = 14049516 | url = http://content.apa.org/journals/abn/67/4/371 | doi = 10.1037/h0040525 | citeseerx = 10.1.1.599.92 }} [http://academic.evergreen.edu/curricular/social_dilemmas/fall/Readings/Week_06/milgram.pdf as PDF.] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150404094832/http://academic.evergreen.edu/curricular/social_dilemmas/fall/Readings/Week_06/milgram.pdf |date=2015-04-04 }}</ref> Czech historians [[Jan Boris Uhlir]] and [[Marie Michlova]] were consulted to check the accuracy of historical aspects of the series.

==The series==
The series aimed to illustrate how life was for ordinary [[Czechs]] during [[German occupation of Czechoslovakia|the occupation]].<ref name="lyman"/><ref name="park"/> The family was chosen according to a "rigorous audition process",<ref name="park"/> from over 600 interested in participating,<ref name="lyman"/> and the eight episodes were filmed during summer 2014.<ref name="guardian"/> There was no competition element to the show, but this element was suggested for the purposes of the main sponsor.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} The family was paid 1 million crowns as motivation and compensation for lost salaries. An isolated farmhouse in the [[Beskydy Mountains]] was redecorated in period style,<ref name="lyman"/><ref name="day"/> with some technical equipment, such as electrical lighting, installed on safety grounds. After a period of settling in and acclimatising to the low rations and other laws of the protectorate, the radio gave information on the developing critical situation regarding [[Nazi Germany]]. The course of the [[World War II in Europe|war in Europe]] was compressed into eight weeks, with a new challenge each week. Situations were created by actors such as the seller (who also brought news and some [[black market]] goods), representing the full scale of characters during the war, including [[Collaboration with Nazi Germany and Fascist Italy|collaborators]], [[Resistance in German-occupied Czechoslovakia|resistance fighters]], German soldiers and the [[Gestapo]]. The situations were written into a coherent story.

==Reception==
Despite a negative reaction from the Czech media, viewing figures were moderate; the first episode was viewed by around 500,000,<ref name="lyman"/> and there were over 1 million downloads from the web page. The show was particularly popular with teenagers.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}} The programme was chosen by the Eurovision Creative Forum Berlin 2015 as one of the 22 most innovative programmes from [[European Broadcasting Union]] member countries in 2015 and was awarded 2nd place.<ref>{{cite web|title=EBU Creative Forum|url=http://www3.ebu.ch/news/2015/09/the-art-forger-named-best-format|publisher=European Broadcasting Union|accessdate=17 September 2015|date=2015-09-15}}</ref> Several foreign broadcasters expressed interest in licensing the format.{{citation needed|date=April 2017}}

==Media response==
Before the show was broadcast, it received international attention due to criticism in the [[Czech Republic]], over its alleged trivialization of the suffering of real wartime survivors,<ref name="park">{{cite news|last1=Park|first1=Dave|title=New Reality Show Puts Czech Family Under Nazi Rule|url=http://www.expats.cz/prague/article/weekly-czech-news/new-czech-reality-show-places-modern-family-under-nazi-rule/|accessdate=22 April 2017|website=Expats.cz|date=19 May 2015|archive-date=5 March 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170305231715/http://www.expats.cz/prague/article/weekly-czech-news/new-czech-reality-show-places-modern-family-under-nazi-rule/|url-status=dead}}</ref><ref name="guardian">{{cite news|title=Czech reality show recreates life under Nazi occupation|url=https://www.theguardian.com/world/2015/may/24/czech-reality-show-nazi-occupation-holiday-protectorate|work=The Guardian|date=24 May 2015}}</ref> including the title of the show describing the Protectorate as a "holiday".<ref name="lyman"/> In response, Cejnkova stated that the program's creators were approaching the topic "with utter seriousness", had intended to open a discussion, and were "aware that it is controversial to return to so turbulent a period", adding that they believed "that it is correct to attempt to do this, providing that certain ethical rules and historical reality are observed."<ref name="day"/><ref>{{cite news|last1=Ghert-Zand|first1=Renee|title=Czech show relives Nazi occupation Critics ask whether 'Big Brother Auschwitz' is next|url=http://www.timesofisrael.com/czech-reality-tv-show-relives-nazi-occupation/|accessdate=13 September 2015|publisher=The Times of Israel|date=21 May 2015}}</ref>

After the first episode was broadcast, the show received more favourable treatment in the press,<ref name="lyman"/> from outlets including the Austrian broadcaster ORF, German TV channels [[ZDF]] and ARD, [[Süddeutsche Zeitung]], [[Associated Press]], [[Reuters]] and [[Prager Zeitung]].<ref>{{cite news|last1=Wiegmann|first1=Katarina|title=Die Serie ist nicht anti Deutsch |trans-title=The series isn't anti German|url=http://www.pragerzeitung.cz/indexphp/home/gesellschaft/19467|accessdate=13 September 2015|publisher=Prager Zeitung|date=3 June 2015}}</ref> ''[[The New York Times]]'' also reported on the show and its critics.<ref name="lyman">{{cite news|last1=Lyman|first1=Rick|title=Grim Reality: Czech TV Makes Game of Nazi Era|url=https://www.nytimes.com/2015/06/06/world/europe/czech-reality-tv-show-makes-a-game-of-life-under-nazi-rule.html|accessdate=13 September 2015|work=The New York Times|date=5 June 2015}}</ref> ''[[The Hollywood Reporter]]'' reported both the criticism of the show and an interview with Cejnkova's response.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Holdsworth|first1=Nick|title=Czech Nazi Occupation Reality Show Draws Criticism|url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/czech-nazi-occupation-show-criticized-797832|accessdate=13 September 2015|work=The Hollywood Reporter|date=25 May 2015}}</ref><ref>{{cite news|last1=Holdsworth|first1=Nick|title=Director of Controversial Czech TV Series Defends Wartime Nazi Occupation Show|url=http://www.hollywoodreporter.com/news/director-controversial-czech-tv-series-797868|accessdate=13 September 2015|work=The Hollywood Reporter|date=25 May 2015}}</ref> However, criticism from some historians and commentators continued, with Mikuláš Kroupa of [[Post Bellum]] saying that the show had "nothing to do with history or telling the stories of that time" and was "just a game".<ref name="lyman"/>

Critics in the Czech press expressed disbelief that people could enter an artificial world and enter the situation as 'real', and remained very critical.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Spáčilová|first1=Mirka|title=Půjčili rodině na Dovolené v protektorátu mobily? Tvůrci to odmítají |trans-title=Did they lend the family from Holiday in the Protectorate mobile phones? The creators deny it|url=http://kultura.zpravy.idnes.cz/kauza-dovolena-v-protektoratu-dzd-/filmvideo.aspx?c=A150603_101215_filmvideo_spm|accessdate=13 September 2015|publisher=iDNES cz|date=3 June 2015}}</ref> Other commentators criticised the critical response itself.<ref>{{cite news|last1=Ferjtek|first1=Thomas|title=Televizní recenzenti na dovolené (v protektorátu)|url=http://www.dokrevue.cz/blog/televizni-recenzenti-na-dovolene-v-protektoratu|accessdate=13 September 2015|publisher=DOC dream|date=9 July 2015}}</ref>
